message:
mcp: run detached audit writer on a native asupersync runtime and add end-to-end regression coverage

The fire-and-forget MCP audit writer in `record_mcp_audit_sync` was
spinning up a `CompatRuntimeBuilder::current_thread()` runtime on
its detached thread. Under the `mcp` feature the goal is to exercise
the native asupersync path end-to-end, but CompatRuntime is the
compat-shim runtime and on tokio-only builds it would transparently
fall back to tokio — hiding native-runtime regressions from the
audit code path in test runs with `mcp` enabled.

Changes in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:

* Move the `runtime_compat::{CompatRuntime, RuntimeBuilder as
  CompatRuntimeBuilder}` import behind `#[cfg(test)]`, since the
  only remaining non-test caller now uses
  `asupersync::runtime::RuntimeBuilder` directly.
* `record_mcp_audit_sync`:
  - Replace `CompatRuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build()` with
    `asupersync::runtime::RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build()`
    on the detached thread. The tracing `warn` message is updated
    to say "native asupersync runtime" so the failure mode is
    identifiable in production logs.
  - Inside the `rt.block_on(...)`, replace the
    `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request)` lookup
    with a direct `crate::cx::for_request()`. This block is running
    on a **detached** thread that owns its own runtime; there is
    provably no ambient Cx to inherit (the caller's Cx cannot cross
    the `std::thread::spawn` boundary), so the audit writer must
    bootstrap a fresh request-scoped capability context. A short
    comment documents the invariant.
* New test-only helper `try_latest_audit_action(db_path,
  action_kind)`:
  - Builds a `CompatRuntimeBuilder::current_thread()` runtime (tests
    still use the compat builder; only the production detached
    thread moved to native asupersync).
  - Opens a `StorageHandle` against the given DB path, issues an
    `AuditQuery { limit: Some(1), action_kind: Some(...) }`, and
    returns `rows.into_iter().next()`.
* New regression test
  `record_mcp_audit_sync_bootstraps_native_runtime_for_mcp_feature`:
  - Calls `record_mcp_audit_sync` with a realistic args payload
    containing `api_key`, `pane_id`, `command`, a `success=true`
    result, and a latency of 42ms.
  - Polls `try_latest_audit_action(&db_path, "mcp.wa.rules_list")`
    with a 25ms sleep and a 5-second deadline. Fails fast with
    `"background MCP audit writer did not persist an audit row
    within 5 seconds"` if the native runtime never ran the
    audit-writing block (the symptom the runtime switch is supposed
    to guard against).
  - Once an audit row arrives, asserts:
    * `actor_kind == "mcp"`,
    * `input_summary` contains `api_key` and `pane_id` but NOT the
      literal secret `sk-secret-123` (i.e. the existing redaction
      still works end-to-end),
    * the stored `decision_context` parses back into a
      `DecisionContext` with `actor == ActorKind::Mcp`,
      `surface == PolicySurface::Mcp`, and `evidence["tool"] ==
      Some("wa.rules_list")`.

No change to the public audit-recording contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
